hooks.text: The PayPal you trust for a 200 dollar order is the same PayPal quietly taking 880 dollars off a 20,000 dollar one. Same app. Very different bill.
PayPal on a small payment barely costs anything. Send a real supplier amount and something changes in the math that nobody warns a business owner about.
PayPal takes 9 dollars on a 200 dollar transfer. On a 20,000 dollar supplier payment that same fee jumps to 880 dollars, before the FX cut.
A business owner used PayPal for years on small orders and felt safe. Then one 20,000 dollar supplier payment showed what that comfort actually costs.
Every 20,000 dollar supplier payment a business owner sends by PayPal quietly loses around 880 dollars in fees, plus an FX cut, before goods even ship.
